title: Reflexion: Language Agents with Verbal Reinforcement Learning
snippet: Framework enabling LLMs to learn through linguistic feedback rather than weight updates. Agents verbally reflect on task feedback signals and maintain reflective text in episodic memory. NeurIPS 2023.

metadata.llm_reason: The seed paper itself - foundational reference for the citation query

title: Robust Verbal Reinforcement Learning for Language Agents
snippet: Enhances Reflexion using vector-backed memory for scalable retrieval and quality-filtering pipelines to prevent contaminated reflections, achieving 82% accuracy on HotPotQA and 97.01% success on AlfWorld.

metadata.llm_reason: Directly extends Reflexion with improved memory architecture

title: MemOrb: Plug-and-Play Verbal-Reinforcement Memory Layer
snippet: Lightweight memory layer distilling multi-turn interactions into strategy reflections for customer service agents, achieving 63 percentage-point gains in multi-turn success rates without fine-tuning.

metadata.llm_reason: Directly builds on Reflexion verbal reinforcement concept for applied domain

title: OPTAGENT: Optimizing Multi-Agent LLM Interactions
snippet: Multi-agent verbal reinforcement learning framework dynamically constructing collaboration structures with feedback mechanisms evaluating communication robustness and coherence for reasoning tasks.

metadata.llm_reason: Extends verbal reinforcement to multi-agent collaboration

title: FinCon: Synthesized LLM Multi-Agent System with Conceptual Verbal Reinforcement
snippet: Multi-agent framework using conceptual verbal reinforcement with manager-analyst hierarchies for financial decision-making, incorporating self-critiquing mechanisms to update investment beliefs.

metadata.llm_reason: Applies Reflexion verbal reinforcement to financial domain with multi-agent design

title: MAR: Multi-Agent Reflexion Improves Reasoning Abilities in LLMs
snippet: Structured multi-agent extension of Reflexion incorporating diverse reasoning personas and a judge model. Improves HotPotQA exact-match by 3 points and HumanEval pass@1 from 76.4 to 82.6.
query: papers building on Reflexion framework self-reflection LLM agent 2025 2026

metadata.llm_reason: Direct multi-agent extension of Reflexion with benchmark improvements

title: Experiential Reflective Learning for Self-Improving LLM Agents
snippet: Experiential memory framework building a pool of reusable heuristics from reflecting on past experience. Uses Reflexion algorithm for collecting success and failure experiences. Published at ICLR 2026 MemAgents Workshop.
query: papers building on Reflexion framework self-reflection LLM agent 2025 2026

metadata.llm_reason: Directly uses Reflexion as base algorithm and extends it with experiential memory

title: Self-reflection enhances large language models towards substantial academic response
snippet: Dual-loop reflection method inspired by metacognition where the LLM critiques its own reasoning process. Reflections stored in a reflection bank. Published in Nature npj Artificial Intelligence.
query: papers building on Reflexion framework self-reflection LLM agent 2025 2026

metadata.llm_reason: Extends Reflexion concept of self-reflection to academic response generation

title: Self-Reflection in LLM Agents: Effects on Problem-Solving Performance
snippet: Empirical study examining the effects of self-reflection in LLM agents on problem-solving, investigating when reflection helps and when it hurts.
query: Reflexion Noah Shinn citation follow-up work self-reflection LLM agents

metadata.llm_reason: Directly studies the Reflexion mechanism empirically

title: CORE: A cognitive science-inspired framework for task-adaptive reasoning enhancement in LLMs
snippet: Cognitive science-inspired framework for task-adaptive reasoning enhancement in large language models. Cites Reflexion.

metadata.llm_reason: Recent 2026 paper citing Reflexion for reasoning enhancement

title: Trace2Skill: Distill Trajectory-Local Lessons into Transferable Agent Skills
snippet: Framework enabling LLM agents to acquire domain-specific skills through analyzing execution experience, demonstrating transferability across different model scales and settings.

metadata.llm_reason: Extends Reflexion idea of learning from execution traces to skill acquisition

title: Natural-Language Agent Harnesses
snippet: Externalizes agent control logic as portable executable artifact with portable natural language expression and shared runtime execution. Cites Reflexion.

metadata.llm_reason: 2026 paper on agent harnesses citing Reflexion for control logic externalization

title: MARCH: Multi-Agent Reinforced Self-Check for LLM Hallucination
snippet: Multi-agent framework addressing confirmation bias through information asymmetry where the verification agent operates independently from original generation.

metadata.llm_reason: Multi-agent self-checking builds on Reflexion self-reflection idea

title: Language Agent Tree Search (LATS) Unifies Reasoning, Acting, and Planning in Language Models
snippet: General framework synergizing LM capabilities in reasoning, acting, and planning. Combines reflection/evaluation and Monte-Carlo tree search. Builds on Reflexion.
query: Reflexion-inspired agent frameworks LATS tree search

metadata.llm_reason: LATS directly builds on Reflexion adding tree search for better exploration
